summary.verdict: FAIL — 5/19 first-green (26.3%) is below the preregistered 50% floor, so the FAIL band triggers on green rate alone, and the rerun lands deeper in the band than round 1 (45%). The second dimension again decisively passes: par form/knob choice is 5/5 among green (and 18/19 overall), so the catalog design remains validated while document-compilability failed harder. The failure signature has changed in kind: round 1 reds were dominated by invention into perceived gaps (invented ops, the entry-accessor dead end); rerun reds are dominated by writers violating the very rules the M5 fixes added (loop/break in 6 of 14 reds despite M5-FIX-6's explicit prohibition) because the fixed text closes the illegal spellings without demonstrating the legal replacements — the sanctioned iteration path (protocol op + conformer) is unwritable since neither grading document shows the FN-3 conform declaration, and the CAT-5a(iii) verbatim-row rule contradicts CAT-5a(ii) coverage (OPEN-FLAG EFFECT-JOIN), wiping out the sweep area 0/4. Prohibition-only fixes moved the failure from invention to unreachable sanctioned paths; the diagnosis is again spec-text wording, not writer inability or catalog design, but per the bands this is recorded as FAIL, not regraded.
summary.green_rate: 5/19 (26.3%) first-green, FAIL band (<50%); per area: entry 0/3, lru 4/4, sweep 0/4, pipeline 1/4, seq 0/4. Prior: 9/20 (45%), per area entry 0/4, lru 4/4, sweep 1/4, pipeline 1/4, seq 3/4.
summary.par_rate: 5/5 (100%) par form/knob among green — clears the 80% par-of-green bar in isolation; 18/19 (94.7%) across all solutions (sole miss: ent-1 hand-indexed traversal instead of the pinned seq_for_each iteration shape). Consistent with round 1 (9/9, 18/20): par choice is not and has never been the failing dimension.
delta_vs_prior: IMPROVED — invented-op class (round 1's #1, 10+ tasks): largely gone. The closed scalar-op fix landed; ieq/ige/ilt/iadd.wrap are now written and graded as legal kernel ops (round 1's ilt grader error class is extinct), and only 2 rerun tasks contain invented callees (pip-1 flagged placeholders, seq-1 next_span).

IMPROVED — entry occupied-path dead end (round 1's #2, all 4 entry tasks): the M5-FIX-1 sanctioned tbl_contains-then-tbl_get_uniq fallback is now known and used (ent-3 followed it exactly and the doc tension was not counted against the writer). Entry stayed 0/3, but for different reasons (loop/break, signature bracket order, hasher abstraction) — the accessor gap itself no longer produces defects.

IMPROVED — lru/worked-choreography transfer: 4/4 green both rounds, and rerun lru defects degraded to purely cosmetic (citation label, prose mischaracterization, one flagged place-grammar extrapolation). Where tasks map onto attested card examples, the fixed text is sufficient.

PERSISTED/WORSENED — closed statement list (M5-FIX-6): loop/break still shipped in 6 of 14 reds (ent-1, ent-2, swp-2, swp-3, pip-1, pip-2) plus // comments in 2. The fix states the prohibition but no document shows the legal iteration spelling: the sanctioned path (seq_for_each/seq_drain/tbl_retain + conformer) requires a FN-3 conform declaration that appears in neither grading document, so the alternative was unwritable; the one green pipeline task (pip-3) succeeded via self-recursion instead.

PERSISTED — region/borrow discipline (M5-FIX-3 a/b/c): still ~9 tasks. Writers now cite and self-flag the rules (the sentences did land as knowledge) but violations continue at exactly the points with no worked example: minting &uniq from an owned local for a call argument (ent-2, swp-2, pip-1, pip-4, seq-1), unbound region names incl. in effect rows (ent-2, pip-2, seq-4, pip-4), uniq-to-shared on rows with no _uniq variant (swp-3, pip-2), fresh-region annotation of receiver-fixed loans (ent-1, swp-3).

PERSISTED — call-site grammar (M5-FIX-5/CAT-1a): receiver-fixed generics respelled at calls in swp-4, swp-2, pip-2; region argument at a non-loan-minting call in pip-4. The prose note did not transfer; no negative example exists in the text.

PERSISTED/UNRESOLVED — effect rows (CAT-5a + OPEN-FLAG EFFECT-JOIN): now the single most damaging class (swp-1, swp-2, swp-3, swp-4, seq-1, plus coverage gaps in ent-2, pip-1, pip-2). The (iii) copy-the-contract-row-verbatim rule and the (ii) caller-coverage rule contradict each other for row-less contract members, so conformer rows are unwritable either way — this open flag alone accounts for sweep going 1/4 to 0/4.

PERSISTED — brand-vs-region (M5-FIX-7/CQ-2): the sentence exists but user-fn brand binding remains unspellable (OPEN-FLAG BRAND-MINT); pip-1 violated it, pip-3 hit the gap and was graded green only because no correct spelling exists.

NEWLY EXPOSED (not covered by round-1 fixes): FN-3 conform grammar absent (swp-1, seq-1, and implicated in every loop/break red); fn-level const-generic binder spelling unattested/unbound (ent-1, swp-3, seq-2, seq-3); kernel prelude Err field name 'error' unsurfaced (pip-1, seq-3); GRAM-11 named-args-for-user-fns outside both documents (swp-2, seq-1, seq-2); mandatory param modes and one-line header canonical form (seq-3, seq-2); generics-before-regions bracket order (ent-3); set on a bare local unattested (ent-1, ent-2, swp-3 — coupled to the iteration gap since accumulators need it once loops are outlawed).
remaining_defects: Iteration is unwritable as documented: M5-FIX-6 (cards.md) closes the statement list and rejects loop/break, but the sanctioned replacement — protocol ops seq_for_each/seq_drain/tbl_retain with a conformer — depends on the FN-3 conform declaration, shown in neither cards.md nor optables.md. Responsible: cards.md M5-FIX-6 + missing FN-3 surface in both catalog documents. Hit: ent-1, ent-2, swp-2, swp-3, pip-1, pip-2 (and swp-1, seq-1 for the conform half).

Conformer effect rows are contradictory: CAT-5a(iii) 'copy the member's declared row verbatim, adding nothing' vs CAT-5a(ii) caller-coverage, unresolved OPEN-FLAG EFFECT-JOIN, with all S.1/S.2 contract members declaring no row. Responsible: optables.md CAT-5a. Hit: swp-1, swp-2, swp-3, swp-4, seq-1.

Borrow minting from owned locals has no worked example: rows spelled &'r/&uniq take borrow_expr atoms with a bound region, but no document shows region 'x { let l = &uniq 'x v; op(l) } for a call argument, so writers pass bare own bindings (OWN-1 hard error) or invent coercions. Responsible: cards.md C1 M5-FIX-3(a)/(b) — normative sentences without a positive example. Hit: ent-2, swp-2, pip-1, pip-4, seq-1, seq-2 (bare affine returns/args).

Effect-row region names and coverage: free region names in rows (seq-4 both fns, pip-2), callee-row coverage omissions (ent-2, pip-1, pip-2), non-canonical multi-region clause (seq-3). Responsible: optables.md CAT-5a(ii)/(iii) + cards.md EFF-1 precedent being example-only.

Call-site generic/region argument discipline: receiver-fixed generics respelled (swp-4, swp-2, pip-2), region argument at a row with loan column NONE (pip-4). Responsible: CAT-1a/M5-FIX-5 — prose rule with no struck-through negative example.

Const-generic and fn-signature surface grammar unattested in the two documents: fn-level const binder spelling and case (ent-1, swp-3, seq-2, seq-3), generics-before-regions bracket order (ent-3), mandatory param modes (seq-3), one-line header form (seq-2), lowercase hasher as non-abstractable keyword set (ent-3). Responsible: kernel GRAM-2/FORM-2/FORM-3/CONST-1 rules absent from cards.md/optables.md, whose signature columns are CAT-1 meta-notation.
Kernel prelude surface not quoted: Result's Err field is 'error', writers wrote 'value' (pip-1, seq-3). Responsible: PRE-1 not reproduced in either grading document.
User-fn call convention: GRAM-11 named-in-declared-order arguments unknown to writers (swp-2, seq-1, seq-2). Responsible: rule lives only in the kernel spec.

Brand binding across fn boundaries unspellable: CQ-2/M5-FIX-7 forbids brand-as-region-parameter but no legal alternative is documented (OPEN-FLAG BRAND-MINT). Responsible: optables.md S.3 + cards.md M5-FIX-7. Hit: pip-1, pip-3 (graded green only because unspellable), pip-4 adjacent.
set on a bare local (accumulator rebinding) unattested — every attested set targets a deref/index place. Responsible: cards.md C1/C2 examples; coupled to the iteration gap. Hit: ent-1, ent-2, swp-3.
next_fixes: Adjacent to M5-FIX-6 in cards.md, add one complete worked iteration example: a conform declaration (conform CountEnv : SeqVisit<u32> { visit = count_visit; }) plus the seq_for_each call and a seq_drain/SeqSink drain, with the sentence: 'v0 iteration is spelled exactly two ways — a protocol op with a conformer, or self-recursion. There is no loop statement and no other spelling.'

Resolve OPEN-FLAG EFFECT-JOIN with one consistent CAT-5a paragraph: either give every S.1/S.2 contract member an explicit effect row that conformers copy verbatim, or replace (iii) with 'a conformer declares exactly the row its body requires; the op's join(F) clause covers it' — and show one worked conformer whose row is nonempty.

Under M5-FIX-3(a), add a positive borrow-minting example: region 'x { let l = &uniq 'x buf; seq_push(l, move v); } with the sentence: 'a row spelled &'r or &uniq 'r never accepts a bare own binding; write the borrow_expr atom, binding the region with a region block when the value is a local.'
Reproduce the kernel prelude Option/Result declarations verbatim in cards.md (Ok(value: T); Err(error: E)) with one match example binding Err(error: e).

Add a fn-level const-generic worked header (fn f<const n: u64>['e](s: &uniq 'e seq<u8, n>) ...) with two sentences: const gparams are lowercase idents; the N/T/K/h in op-table rows are CAT-1 meta-notation and are never written in a fn_decl or respelled at a call.
Add the GRAM-11 sentence with one example: calls resolving to a user fn write named arguments in declared order (f(line: l, from: i)); positional operands are for catalog ops only.

Under CAT-1a/M5-FIX-5, add struck-through negative examples beside the legal call: seq_push<u64, 0>(s, move v) REJECTED / seq_push(s, move v) OK; region arguments appear only at rows with a dedicated result-region parameter (pool_entry pattern), never at rows whose loan column is NONE.

Resolve BRAND-MINT in text: state the one legal way to move cq endpoints across a fn boundary (or state plainly that in v0 endpoints must be consumed in the scope that minted the brand and helper fns must receive them pre-borrowed), and show the compilable helper-fn header for cq_tx_clone.

Add one sentence on set: the legal set places are exactly deref(p).field and index<T>(...) (plus deref(p) if that is intended — decide and say so); a bare local is never a set target; cross-iteration accumulators are spelled as recursion parameters.

State the fn header surface rules the graders drew from the kernel: generics bracket before region bracket, every param carries a mode, header through '{' on one line, reads('a, 'b) as one clause — each with a single worked polymorphic header example in cards.md.
caveats: Grader-not-compiler: green/red is document-based adjudication, not a typecheck run. This caveat already materialized in round 1 (the ilt<u64> red was a grader error over a valid kernel op, per D16-R6), and several rerun adjudications (effect-row coverage on local regions, own-receiver loan annotation, deref-place set) sit on underdetermined text where a real checker could rule either way.

Different tasks than round 1 by design (preregistered fresh trial, no regrade), so per-area deltas are confounded with task difficulty: the rerun set skews heavily toward iteration, drains, and conformers — exactly the constructs the fixed text closes without replacement — and seq going 3/4 to 0/4 partly reflects that mix, not pure regression of the text.
Small samples: 19 tasks total, 5 green; the 45%-to-26% drop is directionally informative but statistically weak, and par-of-green rests on n=5 (though it is consistent with round 1's 9/9 and 18/20).

Several reds were arguably unwritable from the two grading documents alone (conform grammar, brand binder, fn-level const binders, GRAM-11), so the green rate mixes writer error with residual documentation gaps; the grading convention was not perfectly uniform on this (pip-3 graded green because its brand spelling has no correct alternative, while swp-1 stayed red despite a flagged conform-grammar gap).
One task fewer than round 1 (19 vs 20; entry ran 3 tasks, not 4).

Band arithmetic is unambiguous despite the caveats: 26.3% is far below the 50% floor, and no plausible regrade of the ambiguous items (at most ~3 flips: swp-1, seq-2, and one region-judgment call) reaches 50%, so the FAIL verdict is robust to grader error.
Per the preregistered protocol this FAIL is recorded as FAIL; the wording-level fixes above are inputs to a fresh trial, not a rescore of this one.

defects: Uses `loop { ... }` and `break;` — cards.md line 1 [M5-FIX-6] states loop/break are not v0 surface statements and 'a program using them is rejected'; even under kernel-spec grammar (GRAM-5 lines 95-96) loop/break require a LABEL (`loop @l` / `break @l;`), so the unlabeled spelling is malformed there too. The entire traversal does not compile.

Wrong traversal form: the documents' sanctioned iteration is the CAT-5 protocol op `seq_for_each` with a SeqVisit<u32> conformer (CG-ITER: monomorphized must-inline visitor, single-latch loop); an indexed hand loop is not a documented form and also re-pays per-element seq_get bounds machinery instead of the pinned iteration shape.

`set i = i2;` sets a bare local binding — neither grading document attests `set` on anything but a deref/index place (all card examples: `set deref(q).tail = ...`, `set index<u64>(...) = ...`); part of the illegal loop scaffolding.

Region annotation of receiver-fixed loans contradicts [M5-FIX-3](c): `tbl_get_uniq` 'returns its loan at the receiver's region, never a fresh one', but the solution binds it as `&uniq 'w u64` under a freshly minted `region 'w` (same pattern for seq_get as `&'v`). Writer flagged the owned-receiver ambiguity, but the annotation as written names a region the row does not issue.

Effect row `reads, writes` without region arguments is an unattested spelling — every attested fn row parameterizes (`reads('i), writes('c), ...` per C1 and [CAT-5a]); writer flagged this as best-effort. Ambiguous/cosmetic.
fn-level `<const N: u64>` binder is unattested in either document (only the enum-level `const IN: u64` on CqBatchOut exists); writer flagged it as inferred. Minor.

Not defects (verified legal): identity hasher for u32 per TBL-2; TBL-8 two-probe occupied path followed exactly with the second trap elided by the HAS fact; tbl_insert on the absent path; ige/ieq/iadd.wrap are legal kernel ops per [CAT-7a] and spec OP tables; the defensive `check False() else trap` on the Some arm matches the C1 idiom; loan/freeze choreography across ids and t is otherwise sound.

Uses `loop { ... break; }` for the drain: cards.md [M5-FIX-6] states v0 surface statements are exactly fn/let/match/region/set/check/doc/return/move and that loop/break programs are rejected — hard compile reject; the documented legal bulk shape is seq_drain with a SeqSink<Key> conformer per [CAT-5]/S.1 seq_drain row, which the writer never considered or flagged.

`set dup = d2` rebinds a plain let-bound local; every documented `set` targets a place expression through a loan (C2: `set deref(q).tail = t2`, `set index<u64>(...) = v`); local rebinding is unshown and the cross-iteration counter mutation depends on the illegal loop anyway.

Call-site loans `&uniq rest`, `&uniq out`, `&e` are minted on plain owned locals with no bound region name, violating C1 [M5-FIX-3](a) (every region name bound by fn gparam or region 'x { }); no document shows a fresh loan taken from an owned local (writer self-flagged, assumption 3).

`tbl_entry(&uniq out, move k)` writes `move` on the key while assuming copy key_kind; TBL-1 copy KOWN is bare `k: K` (not an own operand), so the move spelling mismatches the row under the writer's own assumption 1.
`let e: own entry<Key, unit>` omits the row's region parameter (token type is entry<'e, K, V>); unverifiable spelling, self-flagged (assumption 5).

Declared effect row `allocates(heap), traps` omits the writes clauses of callees seq_pop (writes('e)) and entry_fill (writes('e)); [CAT-5a](ii) requires caller coverage of callee rows — minor/uncertain since the fn has no region gparams to name and no example settles local-region effects.
Cosmetic: DedupOut declares an unused generic parameter N (its only field besides dups is table<Key, unit, fold>, which does not mention N).

Signature bracket order reversed: `fn bump['e]<K, h>` puts region_params before generics; kernel fn_decl grammar is `"fn" IDENT generics? region_params?` and every polymorphic row in optables.md (e.g. `seq_drain <F: SeqSink<T>>['e, 'v]`) spells type binders before the region bracket [CAT-1; kernel grammar]. The declaration would not parse.

`h` declared as a fn type generic is illegal: kernel `gparam := TYPEID (":" TYPEID)? | "const" IDENT ":" type` admits no lowercase IDENT, and TBL-2 defines h as a closed hasher keyword set (fold/sip_keyed/identity/crc, 'always written') with no documented way for a fn to abstract over it — a concrete hasher spelling was required.

Cosmetic: the impossible-arm trap message 'key appeared between the contains probe and the insert' describes an interleaving the `&uniq 'e` exclusive receiver rules out; the defensive `check False() else trap` arm itself follows the cards C1 precedent, but the message misstates the broken invariant.

Not counted against the writer (doc conflict, correctly flagged in the solution): calling shared-receiver `tbl_contains ['r](t: &'r table...)` on a `&uniq` binding conflicts with cards region discipline (b) (no uniq-to-shared coercion), but TBL-8's normative sanctioned fallback [M5-FIX-1] literally prescribes `tbl_contains(t, k)` then `tbl_get_uniq(t, k)` on the same receiver; the writer followed the sanctioned idiom and surfaced the tension.

set deref(cap) = new_cap writes through a bare (non-projected) dereferenced place; every card-attested `set` targets a field projection (`set deref(q).tail = t2;`) or an index place (`set index<u64>(deref(q).buf, slot) = v;`), so this exact place form is an extrapolation of the place grammar rather than an attested row — the writer flagged it explicitly, and no attested alternative spelling exists for updating the stored capacity field under the card's disjoint-field-borrow posture (cards.md C1 lines 32-36, 193-194; grammar-attestation gap only)

Mis-citation of source file: the solution attributes [TBL-8] to 'cards.md, table' but the rule lives in optables.md S.2 (line 147); the quoted text itself is verbatim-accurate, so this is a citation label error only.

Justification prose mischaracterizes the pool_entry freeze as 'only a shared freeze': per cards.md C1 loan/freeze choreography, np is fully frozen for every pool row while any entry loan (shared OR unique) is live — the shared/unique distinction is the mode of the issued loan, not a weaker freeze. Code is unaffected (no pool row is called inside region 'v), so this is a prose-only defect.

Conformer effect row violates CAT-5a(iii): TblRetain::keep declares no effect row, and a conforming fn must copy the member's declared row verbatim 'adding nothing'; keep_fresh adds reads('v), so FN-3 exact-match conformance (per OPEN-FLAG EFFECT-JOIN) rejects it and the F: TblRetain<K, V> bound at tbl_retain is unsatisfied. The row-less spelling was demonstrably writable: cards.md ring_push/ring_pop read through &uniq 'q while declaring only writes('q).

Conformance is never actually bound: the fn is named keep_fresh (contract member is keep) and no conform declaration (kernel FN-3: conform RetainEnv : TblRetain<u64, Window> { keep = keep_fresh; }) is supplied, so RetainEnv does not conform to TblRetain and tbl_retain(t, f) does not typecheck. Mitigating: the writer explicitly flagged that neither source document shows the conformance grammar rather than inventing one.

Cosmetic: the anti-tbl_drain justification cites the TBL-6 bytes-key HAS-fact consumption mechanism, inapplicable to this solution's copy u64 key; the conclusion still holds because the tbl_drain row kills HAS(t, *) for all key kinds, but the cited mechanism is wrong.

Not counted against green: the writer's second flagged gap (minting &uniq 'v env) is in fact legal kernel grammar (borrow_expr := "&uniq" REGIONID place, an atom per GRAM-9), so the flag was unnecessary; ilt<u64> is a legal kernel compare op; identity hasher is legal for copy integer K per TBL-2 and the assumption was flagged.

close_all uses `loop { ... break; ... }`: cards.md [M5-FIX-6] closes the v0 statement list to fn/let/match/region/set/check/doc/return/move and states loop/break programs are rejected; even kernel GRAM-4 requires labeled `loop @l`/`break @l;`. The documented close-one-by-one shape is seq_drain with a SeqSink conformer.
`DrainSink(out: seq_new<Session, 0>())` nests a call in a construct-field atom position — hard error citing GRAM-9 (three-address/ANF); the seq_new result must be let-bound first.

`seq_push<Session, 0>(...)` and `seq_pop<Session, 0>(s)` respell receiver-fixed T/N at the call — CAT-1a: receiver-fixed generics 'are never respelled or reordered at the call'; the worked example is `seq_push(s, move v)`.

`tbl_drain(sessions, sink)` and `seq_pop(s)` pass bare own affine bindings where the rows take `&uniq`: OWN-1 makes a bare place of affine type a hard error, and GRAM-5/GRAM-9 define borrow_expr (`&uniq 'v sink`, region bound per cards M5-FIX-3(a)) as the argument spelling — the 'undocumented coercion' the writer assumed does not exist; the mechanism is documented and was not used.

`seq_push(deref(env).out, move value)` mints a fresh `&uniq` receiver on a field reached through the live `&uniq 'v` env loan — a reborrow, which cards C1 M5-FIX-3(b) forbids ('v0 has no reborrowing'); the sanctioned through-loan mutations are `set deref(env).field = ...` or ops whose receiver is the loan binding itself.
`move deref(sink).out` derefs an own binding: TYPE-7 defines deref only for &/&uniq/box/arena types; owned-struct field access is `sink.out` (C1 precedent `dead.key`).
`writes('local)` on close_all names an unbound, invented region — cards M5-FIX-3(a)/OWN-3: every region name must be bound by a fn gparam bracket or an enclosing region block.
`let s: own seq<Session, 0> = drained;` uses the affine `drained` bare (OWN-1 requires `move`), and the rebinding is superfluous.
`session_close(move sess)` uses positional arguments on a user fn — GRAM-11 requires named arguments (`param: move sess`); minor since the fn is a flagged out-of-catalog extern.

Ambiguous, doc-internal tension: take's declared row `writes('v), allocates(heap), traps` conflicts with CAT-5a(iii)'s interim rule that a conformer copies the contract member's declared row verbatim 'adding nothing' (TblSink::take declares no row), though CAT-5a(ii) coverage then conflicts the other way (OPEN-FLAG EFFECT-JOIN).
Missed performance knob (cosmetic): the destination seq is built with seq_new instead of presizing via tbl_len + seq_with_cap, so the drain pays avoidable growth reallocations.

Uses `loop`/`break` for the prefix scan: cards.md [M5-FIX-6] lists the exact v0 surface statements and states loop/break 'are not v0; a program using them is rejected' — the program cannot compile; the documented legal scan shape was a counting conformer over seq_for_each_uniq (CAT-5 iteration default)

Calls shared row `seq_len(s)` on a `&uniq 'e` receiver: cards.md borrow rule (b) [M5-FIX-3] forbids using an `&'r` row on a uniq binding and seq_len has no _uniq variant — the writer applied this very rule to seq_get but violated it here

Types the `seq_get_uniq` result as `&uniq 'g` inside a fresh `region 'g`: rule (c) [M5-FIX-3] says a single-region row returns its loan at the receiver's region ('e), never a fresh one; the C1 pool_entry analogy fails because pool_entry has a dedicated result-region parameter and seq_get_uniq does not

Conformer `take` adds `writes('v)` to its effect row: [CAT-5a](iii) requires a contract-conforming fn to copy the contract member's declared row verbatim, adding nothing — SeqSink's member declares no effect row; the solution also redeclares `contract SeqSink<T>`, which optables.md S.1 already provides as normative bytes
Unbound const generic `N` in the fn signature: gparam brackets in every documented example bind only regions; nothing binds N for `seq<Event, N>`
Caller effect row omits the reads clause required to cover seq_len's `reads` row per [CAT-5a](ii) (subsumed by the seq_len illegality)
`set idx = idx2` on a bare local is undemonstrated in either document (every set example targets a place through deref); writer flagged it but shipped it anyway

Shrink-report is fully correct: seq_drain kills only INB/ELEM (CAP survives), facts give only LEN'(s)=LEN(s)-(b-a), SLACK survives per [CAT-2], spill is one-way per [SEQ-2], and no shrink-to-fit row exists — no defect on this half

Call-grammar violation [CAT-1a]/[M5-FIX-5]: `tbl_retain<u64, u64, fold>(t, f)` respells the receiver-fixed generics K, V, h, which 'are never respelled or reordered at the call'; the documented spelling is `tbl_retain(t, f)` (worked example: `seq_for_each(s, f)`). Compile reject, so the code is not green.

Effect-row violation [CAT-5a](iii): conformer `keep` declares `reads('v)`, but the interim rule requires a contract-conforming fn to copy the contract member's declared row verbatim, adding nothing; `TblRetain::keep` in S.2 declares no effect row.

Consequent [CAT-5a](ii) coverage gap: given keep's declared `reads('v)`, `drop_zero_values`'s row `writes('e), writes('v)` fails to cover `join(F) = reads('v)` from tbl_retain's row `writes('e), writes('v) + join(F)`; moot once defect 2 is corrected.

loop/break used in writer_run and reader_run: cards.md [M5-FIX-6] limits v0 surface statements to fn/let/match/region/set/check/doc/return/move and states loop/break programs are rejected (kernel GRAM-5/6 would additionally require a LABEL, absent here); iteration had to be spelled within the allowed statement set
// line comments in run_pipeline: rejected per [M5-FIX-6] (line comments are not v0; kernel FORM-4 no-comments)
Err(value: fail) twice: kernel prelude declares enum Result<T, E> { Ok(value: T); Err(error: E); } — the Err field is 'error', and GRAM-10 makes a misspelled field name a hard error

Bare affine endpoint bindings passed where rows require &uniq receivers: cq_recv(cur_rx) and cq_send(cur_tx, ...) need explicit borrow_expr atoms (&uniq 'r place, GRAM-5/OWN-4); OWN-1 makes a bare place of affine type a hard error
Missing move on affine let initializers (let cur_rx ... = rx; let cur_tx ... = tx; let cur_st ... = st; let cur_src ... = src;): OWN-1 requires 'move p' to consume an affine value
Effect rows under-cover callees [CAT-5a(ii)]: writer_run/reader_run declare writes('q), traps but the cq_send/cq_recv rows are reads('q), writes('q) — reads('q) missing from both

Brand 'q declared as a fn region gparam and instantiated with the brand at the commented calls (writer_run['q](...)): CQ-2 [M5-FIX-7] says a brand may never instantiate a region parameter; only partially covered by the writer's BRAND-MINT flag
Invented ops/types LineSource, source_next_line, parse_entry: honestly flagged as placeholders, but [CAT-7a] makes a spelling in neither document a hard error
cq_new<Entry, spsc, 10>() bare literal 10 for const K: FORM-5 numeric literals carry a type suffix (the solution itself writes 1_u64 elsewhere) — cosmetic/uncertain

Uses `loop { ... break; }` in both the parser and aggregator loops; cards.md [M5-FIX-6] explicitly lists loop/break as not v0 — 'a program using them is rejected'. Both core loops are unwritable as delivered.
Uses `//` line comments inside code blocks (split_endpoints, parser_run); line comments are likewise rejected per [M5-FIX-6].

`seq_len(acc)` is called with `acc: &uniq 'a seq<u64, 0>`; the row takes `['r](s: &'r seq<T, N>)` and cards.md [M5-FIX-3](b) forbids uniq-to-shared coercion — a `&'r` row requires a shared binding, and no `seq_len_uniq` variant exists. Compile reject.

Receiver-fixed generics respelled at call sites: `seq_pop<u64, 0>(raw)`, `seq_push<u64, 0>(acc, move item)`, `seq_len<u64, 0>(acc)` violate [CAT-1a]/[M5-FIX-5]: receiver-fixed T and N 'are never respelled or reordered at the call' (worked example is bare `seq_push(s, move v)`).

Unbound region name `'a` in `aggregator_run['e](..., acc: &uniq 'a ...)` — gparam bracket binds only 'e; [M5-FIX-3](a) requires every region name bound by the fn gparam bracket or an enclosing region block.

Effect-row coverage violations per [CAT-5a](ii): `aggregator_run` omits `allocates(heap)` though it calls `seq_push` (row: writes, allocates(heap), traps) and omits `reads('a)` though `seq_len` reads its receiver; `split_endpoints` declares no effect row at all while calling `cq_tx_clone` (reads('x), writes('q)).

`split_endpoints` returns `own unit`, so tx0/tx1/tx2/rx all drop at scope exit — per CQ-5 the queue closes and is freed immediately; the endpoints structurally cannot reach the four threads (a struct-returning shape was available even with the thread construct undocumented). Partially mitigated by the writer's explicit flag on the undocumented thread/borrow primitives.
Leftover placeholder `enum CqEndsSet { }` marked 'not real — placeholder removed' left in the deliverable (cosmetic).

Prose misattributes the 'designated hot ops' label to CG-QOP; optables.md gives that label to CG-QBATCH — CG-QOP carries no such designation (cosmetic; the blocking per-item choice itself is defensible).
ParserEnv/AggEnv structs are unused and spell fields with an `own` mode annotation unlike the documented struct field spelling (cards.md struct Entry uses bare types) (cosmetic).

Brand binder spelling: produce['q, 'e] and consume['q, 'e, 'd] bind the queue brand 'q in the fn region-gparam bracket, but [CQ-2]/[M5-FIX-7] says a brand may never instantiate a region parameter and the S.3 op rows leave 'q unbound (receiver-fixed). The docs make user-fn brand generics unspellable (OPEN-FLAG BRAND-MINT/BRAND-EFFECTS), so no correct spelling exists, but the writer flagged two other gaps and missed this one.

Local-to-borrow minting: the fresh own local `batch` is passed bare into fill_batch's &uniq 'b parameter with no worked precedent in either document (writer explicitly flagged this); under a maximally strict [CAT-5a(ii)] reading, fill_batch's writes('b) at a caller-local region has no representable clause in produce's declared row (mitigated by the lru_get local-region-'v precedent).

consume is not tail-recursive: iadd.wrap<u64>(n, m) executes after the recursive call, so stack depth grows one frame per batch round over an unbounded throughput run; an accumulator-parameter shape was available in the same closed statement set. No documented rule violated (v0 pins no tail-call guarantee), practical wart only.
Unused bindings: sent:n and rest:r in produce's two arms and e in consume's Err arm are bound and immediately dropped (copy u64s / empty or unsent seq); legal surfaced drops, cosmetic only.

cq_tx_clone<'x>(tx) writes a region argument at a non-loan-minting call: CAT-1a permits region arguments only at a call whose row has a dedicated result-region parameter (the pool_entry C1 pattern); cq_tx_clone's loan column is NONE and 'x is fixed by the operand, so the legal spelling is cq_tx_clone(tx). Present in both the rejected-shape and correct-configuration snippets.
Free region name 'x: cards.md M5-FIX-3(a) requires every region name to be bound by an fn gparam bracket or an enclosing region block; neither snippet binds 'x.

Operand mode mismatch: cq_tx_clone requires &'x cq_tx<'q, T> (shared borrow) but is passed the own binding tx; M5-FIX-3(b) says a row spelled &'r requires a shared binding and v0 has no coercion, so the correct-configuration code does not compile as written (writer disclosed this as schematic; the compilable shape is a helper fn taking tx: &'x cq_tx<'q, u64>).
Cosmetic: code shown as bare statements outside any fn body, unlike the v0 surface shown throughout cards.md.

seq_for_each's documented bound F: SeqVisit<T> is never satisfied: the writer declared a homemade duplicate contract 'SpanCount' instead of conforming SpanCounter to the normative SeqVisit<T> (optables S.1 normative bytes), and no conform declaration (kernel FN-3: conform SpanCounter : SeqVisit<Span> { visit = count_visit; }) binds count_visit as the visit member — seq_for_each<SpanCounter>(spans, counter) is rejected

Owned binding passed where rows demand a borrow: fill(..., spans) and seq_for_each(spans, ...) pass own seq<Span,6> to &uniq 'e / &'r parameters; kernel GRAM-5 requires an explicit borrow_expr (&uniq 'x spans) with 'x bound by a region block (cards M5-FIX-3(a)), and TYPE-5/TYPE-7 forbid implicit own-to-borrow coercion — hard error as written (writer flagged the gap but the spelling still does not check)

next_span is called but never declared, not even the promised signature — a callee in neither the kernel op table nor a program fn_decl is a hard error (OP-1 name lookup / DIAG-1); the claimed doc gap is also overstated since index<u8> place reads (cards C2) plus the CAT-7a full compare set make byte classification expressible

User-fn calls spelled positionally — fill(line, 0_u64, spans) and next_span(line, from) — violate kernel GRAM-11, which requires named-in-declared-order arguments for user fns (positional operands are for table ops only); rule lives outside the two given documents

count_visit adds writes('v) to the conformer row while citing CAT-5a(iii) 'copies the contract member's declared row verbatim, adding nothing' — SeqVisit's visit member declares no row, so the addition contradicts the cited interim rule (a genuine doc tension under OPEN-FLAG (EFFECT-JOIN), but the 'verbatim' self-justification is wrong on the row)

Cosmetic: bare const type-argument 6 in seq_new<Span, 6>() and the seq<Span, 6> type where FORM-5's mandatory-suffix literal discipline suggests 6_u64; const-targ spelling is underdocumented in the two grading files

OWN-1 spelling: `return top;` in undo_pop returns a bare place of affine type `own Option<Command>`; a bare affine place is a hard error and must be spelled `return move top;` (or `return seq_pop(s);` directly). Semantics intended are correct; checker rejects the spelling.

GRAM-11: the user-fn call `undo_pop<N>(s)` uses a positional argument; calls resolving to a user `fn` must write named arguments in declared order (`undo_pop<N>(s: ...)`). Table-op calls in the solution are correctly positional; this one call site is a user fn and is a hard error as written.

FORM-3/GRAM-2/CONST-1: the const-generic parameter is spelled `N` (uppercase) in program text (`<const N: u64>`, `seq<Command, N>`); a const gparam/targ is an IDENT `[a-z][a-z0-9_]*`, so `N` lexes as TYPEID and does not parse. The op table's `N` is [CAT-1] meta-notation, not fn_decl grammar; mechanical rename to lowercase fixes it.

FORM-2/FORM-1 canonical form: fn headers are wrapped across multiple lines (signature / effect row / `{`); EX-1 and both cards.md examples put the entire header through `{` on one line, and non-canonical formatting is a hard error (never auto-formatted).

Completeness: `Command` is used as a TYPEID but never declared, so the fragment is not an acceptable closed program; mitigated by the writer's explicit and correct note that the two documents leave Command unspecified and it is deliberately opaque.

No substantive defects: no invented ops (seq_is_empty correctly refused and derived from seq_len + kernel ieq per [CAT-7]/[CAT-7a]); all quoted row signatures, own/loan columns, effect rows ([CAT-5a] order and callee coverage), and failure channels (pop empty = None value, push growth trap string) are exact; ieq/ilt-family flagging from the earlier grading round does not apply — ieq<u64> is a legal kernel op with the cards' exact call-site spelling.

Unbound const generic N: fn_decl declares only region params ['h,'p,'e] but uses seq<u8, N>; GRAM-2 requires a generics bracket (<const N: u64>) and CONST-1 rejects an IDENT const that names no in-scope const-generic or named-const — compile reject [DIAG-1].

Missing parameter modes: GRAM-2 param := IDENT ':' mode type with mandatory mode; header: slice<'h, u8>, payload: slice<'p, u8>, and payload_len: u64 all omit 'own' (op-table signature columns are meta-notation per [CAT-1], not fn_decl grammar) — parse error.

Wrong Result variant field: kernel prelude PRE-1 defines Err(error: E), but both the match binder Err(value: e) and the reconstruction return Err(value: e) use 'value' — named-binder/field mismatch (GRAM-8/GRAM-10), compile reject. Writer flagged the uncertainty but the code as written fails.

Fact-death analysis wrong: seq_extend_copy's kill column is 'SLACK dec len(src) (killed when unknown)'; no LEN fact exists on the slice parameters (no op minted LEN(header)=4 or LEN(payload)=payload_len, and the writer's own gap #2 admits no slice-length accessor exists), so SLACK(frame)>=total is KILLED at the first seq_extend_copy — it does not survive as an exact decrement and does not die as the n=0 form at the second call as claimed ([CAT-2], S.1 seq_extend_copy row).

Unlicensed elision claim: [CAT-2] says proof may elide only what a row marks elidable; seq_extend_copy's failure column carries no elidable-by clause (contrast seq_get's 'elidable by INB proof'), and the only documented SLACK-driven elision is CG-PUSH's compare+branch on seq_push — so the claim that both bulk-append 'seq capacity overflow' traps are proof-elided is unsupported, answering the task's central question incorrectly.

Non-canonical effect row: reads('h), reads('p) as two clauses instead of the single multi-region clause reads('h, 'p) per EFF-1's reads(REGIONID+) grammar (cards.md precedent writes('i, 'c)) — cosmetic to rejectable.

send_chunks declares effect row writes('s) with a free region name: 's is bound by no fn gparam bracket and no enclosing region block, violating cards.md C1 [M5-FIX-3](a) as applied to effect rows by optables.md [CAT-5a](iii) — compile reject.

build_and_handoff declares writes('e) with a free region name 'e (no ['e] gparam, no region block) — same [M5-FIX-3](a)/[CAT-5a](iii) violation; the cards' ring_new precedent shows a fn mutating only owned locals carries no writes clause (correct row: allocates(heap), traps).

Cosmetic: send_chunks declares traps though its body (return unit) has no trapping callee — spurious over-declaration; a covering superset is arguably legal under [CAT-5a](ii) but does not match any card precedent.
Cosmetic (prose only): the before-state table gives SLACK(s) as 'whatever remains after 2 pushes, <8'; the row's SLACK dec1 rewrite from SLACK>=8 derives exactly SLACK(s)>=6.
